Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Continued To Nov-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Iris Martin Lopez (Trustee).

The Court stated that it appears the Trust is losing money. Mr. Siracusa explained that Mr. Martin had an Investment Advisor with Wells Fargo Bank, that the new/current Advisor has waived fees from the Trust and is not taking a quarterly commission fee, and that his Client felt it was important for her Father to have a stream of income. Mr. Siracusa also stated that the new Advisor suggested this investment to have a guaranteed stream of income at 5%, that Ms. Lopez thought it would be helpful to her Dad, that it is a four year annuity, that he explained to the Trustee that in the future they will come to the Court for authorization and that Wells Fargo gets a commission for Lincoln. In addition, Mr. Siracusa stated that the Paralegals assist with getting CUSIP numbers, that it is extremely difficult and time consuming to get the CUSIP numbers, that the Probate Referees will not do it and will not accept the Inventory and Appraisals without the numbers, and that this is something they have been doing for a long time. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Malone. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Martin Lopez.

The Court granted the Petition to Authorize Trustee to Enter Into Settlement and to Implement Agreement (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Aug-10-2012 Advanced From Oct-01-12 To Aug-20-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order To Bring Trust Under Continuing Copurt Supervision; For Order Confriming
Off Calendar Aug-21-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Iris Martin Lopez (Petitioner). Karen Thompson in Propria Persona. Richard Thompson (Husband of Karen) also present.

Mr. Siracusa explained the reasons for bringing the Trust under continuing Court supervision and stated that bond should be waived because the new instrument clearly waives bond. Ms. Thompson stated that she has no problem with the petition and that her Father needs help. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.
